title: The Third Sunday after the Epiphany
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
Sermon Date: January 23, 2022
Preacher: Katie Pearson

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

The Third Sunday after the Epiphany falls within the Epiphany season, which celebrates Christ's manifestation and revelation. The Revised Common Lectionary readings for this date would have included 1 Samuel 62:1-8 (or Nehemiah 8:8-10), 1 Corinthians 12:12-31a, and Luke 4:14-21, with the latter containing Jesus' reading from Isaiah in the Nazareth synagogue. This represents the early post-Epiphany period before the Lenten season begins, focusing on Jesus' early ministry and his revelation to his disciples and the world.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was navigating post-pandemic reopening and vaccine discussions in January 2022, with many dioceses still managing variable in-person attendance policies. The Diocese of Colorado, which oversees Saint John's Cathedral, was led by Bishop Kimberly Lucas at this time. There were no major General Convention actions scheduled for this period, but dioceses across the country were grappling with ongoing debates about COVID-19 protocols and return-to-normal church life.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing winter conditions typical for late January in Colorado. The week leading up to January 23 had seen coverage of Colorado's ongoing homelessness crisis and discussions around city services. The Capitol Hill and downtown Denver areas, where the Cathedral is located, were dealing with post-holiday retail vacancy and continued discussion about urban recovery following the pandemic's economic impact on small businesses.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The Supreme Court was hearing arguments on Biden's vaccine-or-testing mandates for large employers (decided later that week against the mandate). The January 6 Committee was actively investigating the 2021 Capitol riot, with hearings and subpoenas dominating political news. The Omicron variant of COVID-19 remained the dominant public health story, with record case numbers in the US even as hospitalizations showed some signs of plateauing. The Winter Olympics in Beijing were scheduled to begin in approximately two weeks, generating sports coverage and geopolitical commentary.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

Russia was massing troops on the Ukrainian border in what would escalate into the full invasion in late February 2022, though this was not yet the dominant international story in mid-January. China's government was preparing for the Winter Olympics while continuing its zero-COVID policies and ongoing surveillance in Xinjiang. The Middle East remained tense with Iran nuclear talks at an impasse and Yemen's humanitarian crisis continuing unabated.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

America remained fractured and anxious in early 2022, with pandemic fatigue setting in alongside ongoing political polarization. The cultural zeitgeist was one of cautious re-emergence mixed with uncertainty—people were returning to public life but at variable rates and with lingering anxiety. Entertainment news was dominated by Oscar nomination speculation, with "Spider-Man: No Way Home" dominating box offices, and the NFL playoff season was in full swing with Super Bowl LVI scheduled for February 13, likely generating interest among Denver's sports-centric population.
